title: Symplectic Dynamics of Integrable Hamiltonian Systems
speaker: Alvaro Pelayo
abstract: I will start with a review the basic notions of Hamiltonian/symplectic vector field and of Hamiltonian/symplectic group action, and the classical structure theorems of Kostant, Atiyah, Guillemin-Sternberg and Delzant on Hamiltonian torus actions. Then I will state a structure theorem for general symplectic torus actions, and give an idea of its proof.

proof_stages.label: Symplectic and Hamiltonian setup
proof_stages.summary: The talk fixes the basic language of symplectic manifolds, Darboux local form, symplectic vector fields, and Hamiltonian vector fields generated by energy functions.

proof_stages.label: Momentum maps and toric rigidity
proof_stages.summary: Torus actions are used to generate vector fields, Hamiltonian actions are packaged by momentum maps, and the Atiyah-Guillemin-Sternberg convexity theorem and Delzant classification establish the rigid toric model.

proof_stages.label: Symplectic torus actions beyond Hamiltonian actions
proof_stages.summary: The speaker explains that symplectic but non-Hamiltonian torus actions are natural, then states classification results for actions with Lagrangian orbits and, in dimension four, a complete list with complex-structure consequences.

proof_stages.label: Four-dimensional integrable and semitoric systems
proof_stages.summary: The talk specializes to four-dimensional integrable Hamiltonian systems, defines integrability via a second commuting integral, reviews regular and non-hyperbolic singular fibers, and introduces semitoric systems as the one-periodic-component case.

proof_stages.label: Semitoric classification invariants
proof_stages.summary: A semitoric system is assigned a finite package of invariants—polygonal data, focus-focus Taylor-series data, position/height data, and twisting-index data—which together classify the system up to symplectic isomorphism.

proof_stages.label: Reconstruction and twisting-index realization
proof_stages.summary: The converse construction theorem realizes prescribed admissible semitoric invariant data, in particular showing that the twisting index is a genuine integer invariant that can take arbitrary values.

proof_stages.label: Quantum spectral inverse program
proof_stages.summary: After formulating quantum integrable systems as commuting semiclassical self-adjoint operators, the speaker states the spectral conjecture that the joint spectrum as h tends to zero determines the classical semitoric system and sketches evidence from computations.

proof_stages.label: Connectivity criterion and future directions
proof_stages.summary: The talk closes with a preliminary theorem that absence of horizontal tangencies in the bifurcation set forces connected fibers, and with open problems on general connectivity, moduli of semitoric systems, spectral rigidity, hyperbolic singularities, and higher dimensions.

examples.name: Rotating sphere and height momentum map
examples.note: The Hamiltonian circle action on S^2 has height as its momentum map, whose interval image is the convex hull of the fixed-point images, motivating the general convexity theorem.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker analyzes the circle rotation on S2, identifies the height momentum map, computes its image, and relates it to the fixed points and their convex hull
